code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Unpack params
    bb_period = params['bb_period']
    bb_std = params['bb_std']
    rsi_period = params['rsi_period']
    rsi_lower = params['rsi_lower']
    rsi_upper = params['rsi_upper']
    
    # Calculate Bollinger Bands using ta
    bb = ta.volatility.BollingerBands(close=df['close'], window=bb_period, window_dev=bb_std)
    bb_width = bb.bollinger_wband()
    bb_ma = bb.bollinger_mavg()
    bb_lower = bb.bollinger_lband()
    bb_upper = bb.bollinger_uband()
    
    # Calculate RSI
    rsi = ta.momentum.RSIIndicator(close=df['close'], window=rsi_period).rsi()
    
    # Initialize signals series
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index, dtype='int')
    
    # Detect squeeze: BB width is at a recent 20-period low (volatility contraction)
    bb_width_rolling_min = bb_width.rolling(window=20).min()
    squeeze = bb_width <= bb_width_rolling_min
    
    # Entry conditions: squeeze just ended (current width > previous width) and RSI confirms direction
    # Long: squeeze ended and RSI > 50 (momentum upward)
    long_entry = squeeze.shift(1) & ~squeeze & (rsi > rsi_upper)
    # Short: squeeze ended and RSI < 50 (momentum downward)
    short_entry = squeeze.shift(1) & ~squeeze & (rsi < rsi_lower)
    
    # Exit conditions: price touches opposite band or RSI crosses back to neutral zone
    # Long exit: price closes above upper band (overextended) or RSI falls below 50
    long_exit = (df['close'] > bb_upper) | (rsi < 50)
    # Short exit: price closes below lower band (overextended) or RSI rises above 50
    short_exit = (df['close'] < bb_lower) | (rsi > 50)
    
    # Position tracking
    position = 0
    for i in range(len(df)):
        if position == 0:
            if long_entry.iloc[i]:
                position = 1
                signals.iloc[i] = 1
            elif short_entry.iloc[i]:
                position = -1
                signals.iloc[i] = -1
        elif position == 1:
            if long_exit.iloc[i]:
                position = 0
                signals.iloc[i] = 0
            else:
                signals.iloc[i] = 1
        elif position == -1:
            if short_exit.iloc[i]:
                position = 0
                signals.iloc[i] = 0
            else:
                signals.iloc[i] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: In NZD/USD, periods of low volatility (Bollinger Band squeeze) often precede directional breakouts; entering after the squeeze resolves with RSI confirmation captures the ensuing momentum, while exiting on overextension or RSI pullback locks in profits.
